codeReview.summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, skill file, commit diff, and the current `src/clients/odds_client.py` / `tests/test_odds_client.py` implementation. The helper math, usage table, and freeze update are present, but two blocking correctness problems remain: the client silently drops every event unless `games` rows were pre-seeded elsewhere, and it defers the only database commit until after the full event loop, so one later failure can roll back earlier successful snapshots and quota updates.

issues.description: `fetch_mlb_odds()` silently `continue`s when it cannot resolve a `game_pk` for an API event. In this repo there is currently no `src/` code that inserts or updates `games` rows, and this feature's own preconditions only include scaffolding/schema/models (`features.json:868-871`). On a fresh database the client therefore returns `[]` for real API responses instead of returning `OddsSnapshot` entries, which misses the stated contract that the client fetch MLB odds.

Snapshot inserts and `odds_api_usage` updates performed inside the event loop (lines 394-404) are not committed until line 406. If any later event request fails at line 352 or later parsing/validation raises, sqlite rolls back the earlier successful writes when the connection exits. One bad event can therefore erase already-fetched odds and undercount monthly usage, breaking both persistence and quota tracking.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared command manifest still does not describe the repo's actual lint workflow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/services.yaml:4-10` defines both `typecheck` and `lint` as `python -m py_compile src/**/*.py`, while the reviewed handoff `C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-19T15-06-02-184Z__t5-odds-api-client__f561d71a-25a2-4072-b211-637dbe4da7dc.json` records `ruff check ...` as the lint command that actually ran and fixed a repo lint issue.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared environment/library notes are missing the odds-client implementation detail that this repo uses the free `/events` endpoint plus per-event `/events/{id}/odds` calls and request-usage headers for quota accounting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/environment.md:22-26` documents only the top-level `/v4/sports/baseball_mlb/odds/` endpoint, while `src/clients/odds_client.py:313-352,394-399` uses `/events`, `/events/{id}/odds`, and the `x-requests-used` / `x-requests-last` headers to track quota.

summary: Review failed. The odds client has the requested math helpers and freeze update, but it currently depends on pre-seeded `games` rows to return anything and can roll back previously fetched snapshots/usage if a later event errors. I also recorded shared-state gaps in the services manifest and environment library.
